title: Kepa fistulosa
intro: Kepa fistulosa, also known as Kupa or Kupa Kupa, is a plant species native to Australia, belonging to the family Rutaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and research has confirmed its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Kepa fistulosa include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infused oil, and it is also used in topical creams and salves to support skin health.
preparations: Kepa fistulosa is a plant species that is traditionally used in herbal medicine. However, there is limited information available on its specific uses or preparations.
